indices.name: KOSPI

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: South Korea's main stock market index, the KOSPI, rose to 5224 points on January 30, 2026, gaining 0.06% from the previous session. Over the past month, the index has climbed 21.23% and is up 107.53% compared to the same time last year.

sources.name: Financial News Korea
sources.original_text: The KOSPI finished at 5,224.36, up 0.06% from the previous session. The index climbed as high as 5,321.68, a gain of 1.92%. This was the first time the KOSPI had ever crossed the 5,300 mark intraday.

sources.name: Seoul Economic Daily
sources.original_text: The Korea Composite Stock Price Index (KOSPI) opened at 5,243.42 on Wednesday, up 72.61 points or 1.40 percent from the previous session.

indices.original_text: The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.06% to close at 5,224 on Friday, extending its rally to reach a fresh record high. Historically, the South Korea Stock Market reached an all time high of 5321.68 in January of 2026.

indices.name: KOSDAQ

sources.name: Asia Business Daily
sources.original_text: According to the Korea Exchange on January 30, the KOSDAQ index soared 25.8% this month compared to the previous month. This is the first time in 25 years, since January 2001, that the KOSDAQ index has surged more than 25% in a single month.

sources.name: Korea JoongAng Daily
sources.original_text: The Kosdaq surged 7 percent in a single day to break the 1,000-point threshold on Monday, reaching its highest level since the dot-com bubble in 2000. The index closed at 1,064.41 on Monday.

indices.original_text: On this day, the KOSDAQ index opened at 1,166.23, up 1.82 points (0.16%) from the previous trading day. The index has been on a seven-day winning streak. KOSDAQ rose 30.89 points (2.73%) to 1164.41, the highest since August 2000.

indices.name: Gold (XAU/USD)

sources.name: Finance Magnates
sources.original_text: Gold crashed 8% to $4,941 and silver plunged 17% to $95 on January 30, 2026, the worst single-day decline since 2013.

sources.name: ABC News / AP
sources.original_text: Gold's price dropped 11%, and silver plummeted more than 30% as tremendous rallies for both metals suddenly halted.

sources.name: Money.com
sources.original_text: Gold futures open today, Jan. 30: $5,151.01 per ounce

indices.note: Major crash on Jan 30 - prices fell from record $5,500+ to around $4,900-$5,000
indices.original_text: Gold crashed 8% to $4,941 on January 30, 2026. Despite the brutal selloff, gold remains up 18% year-to-date. Gold had surged past $5,500 earlier in the week before the crash.

indices.name: Silver (XAG/USD)

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: Silver fell to 84.63 USD/t.oz on January 30, 2026, down 26.91% from the previous day. Over the past month, Silver's price has risen 18.77%, and is up 170.58% compared to the same time last year.

sources.name: Fortune
sources.original_text: At 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time today, silver exchanged hands at $99.04 per ounce. That's a precipitous $20.43 drop from 24 hours ago—but still more than a $67 gain over the past year.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.original_text: Silver plunges 30% in worst day since 1980, gold tumbles as Warsh pick eases Fed independence fear

indices.note: Massive crash on Jan 30 - fell from record $122 to around $84-99. WORST DAY SINCE 1980.
indices.original_text: Silver plunged over 25% toward $84 per ounce Friday. The white metal had surged to a record $122 Thursday. Despite the sharp correction, it remained on track for a monthly gain of more than 30%.

indices.name: Bitcoin (BTC/USD)

sources.name: CoinDesk
sources.original_text: Bitcoin's price continued to pull back in late Thursday evening U.S. hours, tumbling to as low as $81,000 before bouncing to the current $82,000.

sources.name: Bloomingbit
sources.original_text: As of 03:24 on the 31st, BTC was trading at $81,957.13 on Binance's Tether (USDT) market, down 2.46% from the previous day.

sources.name: CoinDesk (DVOL)
sources.original_text: Deribit's bitcoin volatility index, known as DVOL, jumped sharply, rising from around 37 to above 44. Bitcoin is facing additional selling pressure, trading around $82,000 after sliding 7% in the past 24 hours.

indices.note: Major sell-off - fell from $130,000 high in early Jan to ~$82,000. $1.75B in liquidations.
indices.original_text: Bitcoin pulled back to as low as $81,000 as horrendous day continues. The world's largest cryptocurrency has shed nearly $10,000 over the past 24 hours. More than $777 million in leveraged crypto long positions were liquidated in the space of one hour.
